name: Irving Fine
abstract: Irving Gifford Fine (December 3, 1914 – August 23, 1962) was an American composer. Fine's work assimilated neoclassical, romantic, and serial elements.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: It had a very good music department, with an all-star faculty of Harold Shapiro and the very gifted, excellent composer, who died very young, named Irving Fine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
